appraisal_events.evidence: « deux ou trois fois » marquèrent à vif une sorte de croix dans son coeur ... sa jalousie ... le poussait à sa perte ... parvînt à sourire pour dissimuler sa torture.
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator presents Swann as overwhelmed by jealousy and pain triggered by Odette’s words, losing emotional control and being driven to self-harming inquiry.

status_effects.explanation: Swann is locally diminished by acute jealousy and dependence, visibly suffering and driven to press for details despite the pain.
